title: Uruguayan Cheese Empanadas
description: A traditional Uruguayan dish, these flaky empanadas are filled with rich, melted cheese, perfect as a snack or dinner.
instructions: 1. In a large mixing bowl, combine flour and salt.
2. Add cold butter to the flour mixture, and use a pastry blender to work it into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Gradually add water to the mixture, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
4. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface, and knead a few times until smooth.
5. Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces.
6. Roll out each piece of dough into a thin circle.
7. Place 1 tablespoon of grated cheese in the center of each dough circle.
8. Fold the dough over the cheese, and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
9. Use a fork to crimp the edges of the empanada.
10. Brush the tops of the empanadas with beaten egg, and sprinkle with a pinch of salt.
12. Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
